page: OVHcloud l4-90 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9454 processor with 22 dedicated physical cores running at 2.75 GHz. The system is equipped with 90.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 4.09 GB of memory per core. For hardware acceleration, it integrates a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, offering 24 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier performance in several key areas. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top 10%, while multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25%. The server's memory subsystem achieves a top-tier PassMark memory score. In terms of specialized workloads, the NVIDIA L4 GPU delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models, processing 2286.1 prompt tokens per second and 54.36 generation tokens per second for a 7B model. Image processing and physics simulations also register strong performance, falling within the top 25% of benchmarked systems.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the l4-90 offers a highly dense resource profile by pairing dedicated AMD EPYC CPU cores with a modern Ada Lovelace GPU and local NVMe storage. This configuration minimizes latency and avoids the overhead of shared-thread architectures. However, memory bandwidth for large blocks remains in the average tier, which may present a bottleneck for massive out-of-core datasets that exceed the 24 GB VRAM or require rapid host-to-device transfers.

This server is optimized for workloads requiring dedicated compute and hardware acceleration. It is highly suited for LLM inference of small to medium models, image processing, physics simulations, and parallelized data decompression. The inclusion of a dedicated public IPv4 address and 8 Gbps bandwidth also supports real-time API serving and distributed machine learning tasks.
description: OVHcloud l4-90 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server designed for compute-intensive and machine learning workloads. It features an AMD EPYC 9454 processor with 22 dedicated physical cores running at 2.75 GHz, paired with 90.0 GB of RAM and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. Hardware acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM on the Ada Lovelace architecture. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models. It also delivers strong performance in image processing, physics simulations, and multi-threaded decompression. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and dedicated resources, the l4-90 is highly suited for real-time AI inference, image processing, and parallel scientific computing.
og_description: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 22 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 90 GB RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB VRAM, optimized for top-tier LLM inference and image processing.
meta_description: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server with 22 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 90 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, built for AI inference and compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring an NVIDIA L4 GPU and NVMe storage, optimized for high-efficiency AI inference and parallel computing.
bullet_points: Powered by 22 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 cores at 2.75 GHz.
Equipped with 1x NVIDIA L4 GPU featuring 24 GB of VRAM.
Includes 90 GB RAM and a fast 400 GB local NVMe SSD.
Delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models.
Provides 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address.
Best suited for AI inference, image processing, and physics simulations.
